message:
chore(node): update beads issue tracker state

Sync .beads/issues.jsonl with current issue status — reflects newly
created beads for ATC signal extraction and federation work items.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1hj3","title":"[10.19] Implement local signal extraction pipeline from trust cards, adversary graph, and control-plane events.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.19 — Adversarial Trust Commons Execution Track (9M)

Why This Exists:
ATC federated intelligence track for privacy-preserving cross-deployment threat learning, robust aggregation, and verifier-backed trust metrics.

Task Objective:
Implement local signal extraction pipeline from trust cards, adversary graph, and control-plane events.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Extraction is deterministic for identical inputs; sensitive raw payloads are excluded by policy; extraction outputs are replay-auditable.

Expected Artifacts:
- `src/federation/atc_signal_extractor.rs`, `tests/conformance/atc_signal_extraction.rs`, `artifacts/10.19/atc_local_signal_samples.jsonl`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_19/bd-1hj3/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_19/bd-1hj3/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.19] Implement local signal extraction pipeline from trust cards, adversary graph, and control-plane events.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.19] Implement local signal extraction pipeline from trust cards, adversary graph, and control-plane events." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.19] Implement local signal extraction pipeline from trust cards, adversary graph, and control-plane events." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.19] Implement local signal extraction pipeline from trust cards, adversary graph, and control-plane events." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.19] Implement local signal extraction pipeline from trust cards, adversary graph, and control-plane events." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"- Extraction is deterministic for identical inputs; sensitive raw payloads are excluded by policy; extraction outputs are replay-auditable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:05.501718880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T07:07:28.106296211Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T07:07:28.106270343Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-19","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hj3","depends_on_id":"bd-3aqy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1iaf","title":"fix(tools): harden benchmark_suite against non-finite measurements and silent JSON serialization failure","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:33:11.729527925Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-11T07:06:36.101510719Z","closed_at":"2026-03-11T07:06:36.101475122Z","close_reason":"Implemented benchmark_suite hardening; remaining fmt/check/clippy/test blockers were unrelated repo issues","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":38,"issue_id":"bd-1iaf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented benchmark_suite hardening in crates/franken-node/src/tools/benchmark_suite.rs plus main.rs API plumbing. Added fail-closed rejection for empty/non-finite measurement sets, fail-closed handling for non-finite regression thresholds, explicit report-float validation before JSON serialization, and focused regressions. Verification: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check on touched files passed locally; python3 scripts/check_benchmark_suite.py --json passed; rch cargo fmt --check failed on unrelated pre-existing formatting diffs in connector files; rch cargo check --all-targets timed out after ~302s without Rust diagnostics; rch cargo clippy --all-targets reached frankenengine-node but failed on unrelated pre-existing clippy lint in crates/franken-node/src/connector/crdt.rs:195 (map_or simplification); warm-cache rch cargo test -p frankenengine-node benchmark_suite --lib previously ran 40 benchmark_suite tests with 39 pass / 1 fail, where the only failure was the newly added non-finite serialization guard; after fixing that path, a focused rerun was blocked by unrelated upstream compile error in /data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine/src/react_mismatch_catalog.rs:830 (move out of self.catalog_hash).","created_at":"2026-03-11T07:06:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1id0","title":"[10.15] Publish tri-kernel ownership contract (`franken_engine`, `asupersync`, `franken_node`) with explicit interface boundaries.","description":"## Why This Exists
The three-kernel architecture partitions franken_node into three responsibility planes: franken_engine (execution kernel), asupersync (correctness/control kernel), and franken_node (product kernel). Without an explicit, machine-enforceable ownership contract, module boundaries erode — execution logic leaks into the product plane, correctness invariants get bypassed, and the 10 Hard Runtime Invariants from Section 8.5 lose their structural anchor. This bead publishes the foundational tri-kernel ownership contract that every subsequent 10.15 bead depends on: it names which kernel owns each capability (Cx propagation, region lifecycle, cancellation protocol, obligation tracking, scheduler lanes, remote effects, epoch management, evidence ledger, deterministic verification, and authority gating), defines the interface boundaries between them, and makes boundary violations deterministic CI failures.

## What This Must Do
1. Author `docs/architecture/tri_kernel_ownership_contract.md` that:
   - Names the three kernels (franken_engine, asupersync, franken_node) and their responsibility planes (execution, correctness/control, product).
   - Maps each of the 10 Hard Runtime Invariants (8.5) to its canonical owner kernel.
   - Defines the permitted inter-kernel API surface: which types, traits, and function signatures may cross boundaries.
   - Enumerates any current exceptions with time-bounded migration deadlines and signed waiver requirements.
2. Implement `tests/conformance/ownership_boundary_checks.rs` that:
   - Parses the crate dependency graph and module structure under `crates/franken-node/src/`.
   - Asserts that no module in the product plane directly invokes correctness-kernel internals bypassing the defined interface.
   - Asserts that no execution-kernel primitive is re-implemented in the product plane.
   - Fails deterministically with a structured error listing the violating module path, the boundary crossed, and the contract clause violated.
3. Generate `artifacts/10.15/ownership_boundary_report.json` containing:
   - Per-module ownership classification.
   - Boundary crossing audit results (pass/fail per boundary).
   - Exception inventory with waiver status and expiry dates.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Contract names owners for execution, correctness, and product planes; boundary violations have deterministic CI failures; exceptions require signed waiver artifact.
- The contract document is parseable by CI tooling (structured frontmatter with kernel-to-capability mapping).
- Conformance test covers all modules under `crates/franken-node/src/connector/`, `crates/franken-node/src/conformance/`, and `crates/franken-node/src/`.
- Any unsigned or expired waiver causes a hard CI failure, not a warning.
- The ownership report JSON schema is stable and consumed by the section gate (bd-20eg).

## Testing & Logging Requirements
- **Unit tests**: Validate contract document parsing, waiver expiry logic, and boundary classification for synthetic module graphs.
- **Integration tests**: Run ownership_boundary_checks.rs against the real crate tree and assert zero violations (or only waivered ones).
- **Conformance tests**: Inject a synthetic boundary violation (e.g., product module importing correctness-kernel internal) and assert deterministic failure with correct error code.
- **Adversarial tests**: Attempt to bypass the gate with an expired waiver, an unsigned waiver, and a waiver for a non-existent module.
- **Structured logs**: Event codes `OWN-001` (boundary check pass), `OWN-002` (boundary violation detected), `OWN-003` (waiver applied), `OWN-004` (waiver expired/invalid). All entries include trace correlation ID and module path.

## Expected Artifacts
- `docs/architecture/tri_kernel_ownership_contract.md`
- `tests/conformance/ownership_boundary_checks.rs`
- `artifacts/10.15/ownership_boundary_report.json`
- `artifacts/section_10_15/bd-1id0/verification_evidence.json`
- `artifacts/section_10_15/bd-1id0/verification_summary.md`

## Dependencies
- **Upstream**: bd-cda (Execution Normalization Contract), bd-5rh (10.14 FrankenSQLite track), bd-1ow (10.1 Charter + Split Governance)
- **Downstream**: bd-2177 (workflow inventory), bd-33kj (claim-language policy), bd-20eg (section gate)","acceptance_criteria":"- Contract names owners for execution, correctness, and product planes; boundary violations have deterministic CI failures; exceptions require signed waiver artifact.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"NavyPeak","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:59.474000123Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T02:35:54.313802935Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T02:35:54.313774653Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-15","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1id0","depends_on_id":"bd-1ow","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1id0","depends_on_id":"bd-5rh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1id0","depends_on_id":"bd-cda","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-20ir2","title":"policy/hardening_clamps.rs: max_overhead_pct (f64) lacked is_finite() guard. NaN comparisons always return false, silently bypassing the overhead ceiling check and allowing Critical-level escalation (60% overhead) through even when the budget should cap it. Fixed by adding is_finite() guard that fails closed to 0.0 overhead budget.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-03-19T17:55:06.097083540Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T17:55:51.355256097Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T17:55:51.355232664Z","close_reason":"Fixed: is_finite() guard on max_overhead_pct","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-20l","title":"[10.1] Add ADR: "Hybrid Baseline Strategy" codifying no Bun-first clone, spec-first compatibility extraction, and native franken architecture from day one.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.1 — Charter + Split Governance

Why This Exists:
Program charter and governance baseline: split contracts, reproducibility, and anti-regression rules for strategic integrity.

Task Objective:
Add ADR: "Hybrid Baseline Strategy" codifying no Bun-first clone, spec-first compatibility extraction, and native franken architecture from day one.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Implement with explicit success/failure invariants and deterministic behavior under normal, edge, and fault scenarios.
- Ensure outcomes are verifiable via automated tests and reproducible artifact outputs.

Expected Artifacts:
- Section-owned design/spec artifact at `docs/specs/section_10_1/bd-20l_contract.md`, capturing decision rationale, invariants, and interface boundaries.
- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_1/bd-20l/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_1/bd-20l/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.1] Add ADR: "Hybrid Baseline Strategy" codifying no Bun-first clone, spec-first compatibility extraction, and native franken architecture from day one.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.1] Add ADR: "Hybrid Baseline Strategy" codifying no Bun-first clone, spec-first compatibility extraction, and native franken architecture from day one." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.1] Add ADR: "Hybrid Baseline Strategy" codifying no Bun-first clone, spec-first compatibility extraction, and native franken architecture from day one." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.1] Add ADR: "Hybrid Baseline Strategy" codifying no Bun-first clone, spec-first compatibility extraction, and native franken architecture from day one." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.1] Add ADR: "Hybrid Baseline Strategy" codifying no Bun-first clone, spec-first compatibility extraction, and native franken architecture from day one." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:43.680535788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T09:23:59.423500678Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T09:23:59.423474329Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-1","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20l","depends_on_id":"bd-1mj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-20rv","title":"Fix remaining miscellaneous clippy warnings (derive Default, clone-on-Copy, assert_eq bool, etc.)","description":"Fix ~45 remaining mechanical clippy warnings across many categories: new_without_default/derive Default (14), impl can be derived (2), clone on Copy (2), assert_eq bool (2), get().is_some → contains_key (3), sort_by_key (2), clamp (2), push after creation (2), unused imports (2), identical if blocks (2), loop variable indexing (2), constant assertions (2), clone→from_ref (2), plus ~10 singleton warnings.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltCat","created_at":"2026-02-22T20:43:19.500882863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T20:54:19.782764123Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T20:54:19.782740459Z","close_reason":"Fixed ~47 miscellaneous clippy warnings: 16 new_without_default (derive/impl Default), 2 derivable impl, 2 clone-on-Copy, 2 assert_eq bool, 2 get().is_some → contains_key, 2 sort_by → sort_by_key, 2 clamp, 3 vec push-after-creation, 2 identical if blocks, 2 loop variable indexing, 2 constant assertions, 2 clone → from_ref, 1 unused import, 1 redundant closure, 1 abs_diff, plus 10 singleton fixes. Build and all tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-20sxs","title":"Fix trust quarantine demo artifact contract mismatch","description":"CLI/e2e expects trust quarantine --artifact sha256:deadbeef to match the demo trust registry, but demo_registry seeds different artifact hashes so the quarantine path cannot satisfy its own contract. Align the seeded trust-card data or quarantine semantics and add regression coverage.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-01T03:14:06.506893304Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T03:14:14.918588279Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-20uo","title":"[10.14] Integrate proof-carrying repair artifacts into decode/reconstruction paths.","description":"## Why This Exists
Proof-carrying repair is a core 9J enhancement pattern: when franken_node reconstructs data from erasure-coded fragments or repairs corrupted objects, the repair operation itself must emit verifiable proof metadata. Without this, a repaired object is indistinguishable from a potentially-corrupt one, and the system cannot reason about whether reconstruction was faithful. This bead integrates proof artifacts directly into the decode/reconstruction pipeline so that every repair output carries cryptographic evidence of its correctness, enabling downstream trust decisions (quarantine promotion, durable claims) to be evidence-based rather than trust-based.

## What This Must Do
1. Create `crates/franken-node/src/repair/proof_carrying_decode.rs` implementing the `ProofCarryingDecoder` trait/struct.
2. During decode/reconstruction, emit a `RepairProof` structure containing: input fragment hashes, reconstruction algorithm identifier, output hash, and a signed attestation binding these together.
3. Implement a `ProofVerificationApi` that accepts a `RepairProof` and validates: (a) input fragment hashes match stored originals, (b) algorithm identifier is registered, (c) output hash matches recomputed value.
4. In mandatory proof modes (configurable via policy), flag missing proofs as hard errors that prevent the repaired object from being used.
5. In advisory proof modes, log missing proofs as warnings but allow operation to proceed.
6. Integrate with the `DurableClaimGate` (bd-1l62) so that repair completion claims require valid proof metadata.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Repair operations emit proof metadata in required modes; proof verification API validates emitted artifacts; missing proofs are flagged where mandatory.
- ADDITIONAL: Proof metadata round-trips through serialization (JSON) without loss.
- ADDITIONAL: Verification API rejects tampered proof artifacts (modified hash, wrong algorithm ID).
- ADDITIONAL: Mode switching between mandatory and advisory is tested end-to-end.
- ADDITIONAL: At least 3 sample proof artifacts are generated and stored as golden vectors.

## Testing & Logging Requirements
- Unit tests: `ProofCarryingDecoder` emits correct proof for known input/output pairs; `ProofVerificationApi` accepts valid proofs and rejects tampered ones; mode flag toggles behavior correctly.
- Integration tests: Full repair pipeline from fragment retrieval through decode to proof emission and verification; proof metadata persists across restart.
- Conformance tests: Proof format matches published spec; verification is deterministic across platforms.
- Adversarial tests: Inject corrupted fragments and verify proof correctly reflects reconstruction failure; attempt to forge proof without valid inputs.
- Structured logs: Event codes `REPAIR_PROOF_EMITTED`, `REPAIR_PROOF_VERIFIED`, `REPAIR_PROOF_MISSING`, `REPAIR_PROOF_INVALID` with fields: `object_id`, `fragment_count`, `algorithm`, `proof_hash`, `mode`, `trace_id`.

## Expected Artifacts
- `src/repair/proof_carrying_decode.rs` -- core implementation
- `tests/conformance/proof_carrying_repair.rs` -- conformance test suite
- `artifacts/10.14/repair_proof_samples.json` -- sample proof artifacts / golden vectors
- `artifacts/section_10_14/bd-20uo/verification_evidence.json` -- machine-readable CI evidence
- `artifacts/section_10_14/bd-20uo/verification_summary.md` -- human-readable verification summary

## Dependencies
- Upstream:
  - bd-1l62 (Gate durable-claiming operations on verifiable marker/proof availability)
- Downstream (depends on this):
  - bd-3epz (Section-wide verification gate)
  - bd-5rh (Section 10.14 parent tracking bead)","acceptance_criteria":"Repair operations emit proof metadata in required modes; proof verification API validates emitted artifacts; missing proofs are flagged where mandatory.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:56.625163980Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T19:43:45.240884737Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T19:43:45.240853199Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-14","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20uo","depends_on_id":"bd-1l62","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-20z","title":"[PLAN 10.11] FrankenSQLite-Inspired Runtime Systems Integration Track","description":"Section: 10.11 — FrankenSQLite-Inspired Runtime Systems Integration Track

Strategic Context:
FrankenSQLite-inspired systems integration of capabilities, cancellation protocol, obligations, deterministic labs, and anti-entropy.

Execution Requirements:
- Preserve all scoped capabilities from the canonical plan.
- Keep contracts self-contained so future contributors can execute without reopening the master plan.
- Require explicit testing strategy (unit + integration/e2e) and structured logging/telemetry for every child bead.
- Require artifact-backed validation for performance, security, and correctness claims.

Dependency Semantics:
- This epic is blocked by its child implementation beads.
- Cross-epic dependencies encode strategic sequencing and canonical ownership boundaries.

## Success Criteria
- All child beads for this section are completed with acceptance artifacts attached and no unresolved blockers.
- Section-level verification gate for comprehensive unit tests, integration/e2e workflows, and detailed structured logging evidence is green.
- Scope-to-plan traceability is explicit, with no feature loss versus the canonical plan section.

## Optimization Notes
- User-Outcome Lens: "[PLAN 10.11] FrankenSQLite-Inspired Runtime Systems Integration Track" must improve operator confidence, safety posture, and deterministic recovery behavior under both normal and adversarial conditions.
- Cross-Section Coordination: child beads must encode integration assumptions explicitly to avoid local optimizations that degrade system-wide correctness.
- Verification Non-Negotiable: completion requires reproducible unit/integration/E2E evidence and structured logs suitable for independent replay.
- Scope Protection: any simplification must preserve canonical-plan feature intent and be justified with explicit tradeoff documentation.","notes":"Acceptance Criteria alias (plan-space normalization, 2026-02-20):
- The `Success Criteria` section in this bead is the canonical acceptance contract for closure.
- Closure additionally requires linked unit/integration/E2E verification evidence and detailed structured logging artifacts from all child implementation  {"id":"bd-3u2o","title":"[10.16] Add substrate conformance gate in CI to block non-compliant feature merges.","description":"## Why This Exists

Substrate integration policies (bd-2owx) are useless without automated enforcement. This bead adds a CI conformance gate that blocks merges of features that violate the substrate policy — for example, a PR that adds raw terminal output in a frankentui-mandatory module, or persists state without going through the frankensqlite adapter in a mandatory domain. The gate provides remediation hints and a waiver path for justified exceptions.

In the three-kernel architecture, franken_node's substrate integrations must be maintained over time as new features are added. Without CI enforcement, policy drift is inevitable — developers will take shortcuts, and the substrate integration will degrade. This gate prevents that decay.

## What This Must Do

1. Create `.github/workflows/adjacent-substrate-gate.yml` containing:
   - A CI workflow that runs on every PR targeting main.
   - Steps:
     1. Parse the substrate policy manifest (`artifacts/10.16/adjacent_substrate_policy_manifest.json` from bd-2owx).
     2. Identify which franken_node modules are modified in the PR.
     3. For each modified module, check compliance with the substrate policy:
        - If module is frankentui-mandatory: verify no raw `println!`/ANSI codes, verify frankentui imports present.
        - If module is frankensqlite-mandatory: verify persistence goes through adapter, no direct file I/O for state.
        - If module is sqlmodel_rust-mandatory: verify typed models are used, no raw SQL strings.
        - If module is fastapi_rust-mandatory: verify endpoints use the skeleton's middleware pipeline.
     4. Check waiver registry (`artifacts/10.16/waiver_registry.json` from bd-159q) for justified exceptions.
     5. Fail with remediation hints if violations found and no valid waiver exists.
     6. Pass if all modified modules comply or have valid unexpired waivers.

2. Create `tests/conformance/adjacent_substrate_gate.rs` containing:
   - Conformance test functions that implement the same checks as the CI workflow but runnable locally.
   - Tests for each substrate's compliance rules.
   - Tests that verify waiver lookup and expiry checking work correctly.

3. Generate `artifacts/10.16/adjacent_substrate_gate_report.json` containing:
   - `checks[]` array with `{module, substrate, rule, status: "pass"|"fail"|"waived", remediation_hint, waiver_id}`.
   - `summary` with `{total_checks, passed, failed, waived}`.
   - `gate_verdict`: `"pass"` or `"fail"`.

4. Create verification script `scripts/check_substrate_gate.py` with `--json` flag and `self_test()`:
   - Validates gate report completeness.
   - Ensures gate verdict is consistent with individual check results.
   - Verifies remediation hints are non-empty for all failures.

5. Create `tests/test_check_substrate_gate.py` with unit tests.

6. Produce evidence artifacts:
   - `artifacts/section_10_16/bd-3u2o/verification_evidence.json`
   - `artifacts/section_10_16/bd-3u2o/verification_summary.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- CI detects relevant-feature noncompliance with substrate policy; failures include remediation hints and waiver path.
- The CI workflow parses the substrate policy manifest and applies correct rules per module.
- Violations produce actionable remediation hints (not just "failed" — must say what to fix and how).
- Valid unexpired waivers are recognized and do not trigger failures.
- Expired waivers are treated as violations.
- The gate can run both in CI (`.github/workflows/`) and locally (`cargo test` conformance suite).

## Testing & Logging Requirements

- **Unit tests**: Validate policy manifest parsing, module compliance checking, waiver lookup, expiry date comparison, and remediation hint generation.
- **Integration tests**: Simulate PR diffs with policy violations, valid waivers, and expired waivers; verify correct gate verdicts.
- **Event codes**: `SUBSTRATE_GATE_START` (info), `SUBSTRATE_GATE_VIOLATION` (error), `SUBSTRATE_GATE_WAIVED` (info), `SUBSTRATE_GATE_WAIVER_EXPIRED` (warning), `SUBSTRATE_GATE_PASS` (info), `SUBSTRATE_GATE_FAIL` (error).
- **Trace correlation**: PR identifier and gate run ID in all gate events.
- **Deterministic replay**: Gate tests use fixed module content and policy manifests.

## Expected Artifacts

- `.github/workflows/adjacent-substrate-gate.yml`
- `tests/conformance/adjacent_substrate_gate.rs`
- `artifacts/10.16/adjacent_substrate_gate_report.json`
- `scripts/check_substrate_gate.py`
- `tests/test_check_substrate_gate.py`
- `artifacts/section_10_16/bd-3u2o/verification_evidence.json`
- `artifacts/section_10_16/bd-3u2o/verification_summary.md`

## Dependencies

- **bd-2owx** (blocks): The substrate policy manifest must exist before the gate can enforce it.

## Dependents

- **bd-10g0**: Section gate depends on this bead.","acceptance_criteria":"- CI detects relevant-feature noncompliance with substrate policy; failures include remediation hints and waiver path.","notes":"Claimed via bv --robot-plan after closing bd-2owx; implementing CI substrate conformance gate and verifier artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DarkLantern","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:02.600606433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T03:50:19.715850672Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T03:50:19.715819805Z","close_reason":"Implemented CI substrate conformance gate workflow, report generator/validator, conformance checks, tests, and verification artifacts.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-16","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3u2o","depends_on_id":"bd-2owx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3u4","title":"[10.11] Implement BOCPD regime detector for workload/incident stream shifts.","description":"[10.11] Implement BOCPD regime detector for workload/incident stream shifts.

## Why This Exists

Section 9G.5 requires automated detection of regime shifts in operational streams. Workload patterns, incident rates, trust-signal distributions, and error frequencies can shift abruptly due to deployments, attacks, infrastructure changes, or organic growth. Human operators often detect these shifts too late — after SLO violations or security incidents have already occurred. Bayesian Online Changepoint Detection (BOCPD) provides a principled, low-latency mechanism for detecting these shifts as they happen, enabling proactive policy recalibration and early alerting.

## What It Must Do

1. **BOCPD core algorithm.** Implement the Adams & MacKay (2007) Bayesian Online Changepoint Detection algorithm. The implementation must support configurable hazard functions (constant hazard rate is the default, with geometric and custom hazard as options). The posterior over run lengths must be maintained efficiently using the standard message-passing scheme.

2. **Observation models.** Support at least three conjugate observation models:
   - **Gaussian** (normal-inverse-gamma prior): for latency, throughput, and continuous metrics.
   - **Poisson** (gamma prior): for incident counts, error counts, and event rates.
   - **Categorical** (Dirichlet prior): for trust-signal class distributions and error code distributions.
   Each model must support online parameter updates without storing the full history.

3. **Changepoint signaling.** When the posterior probability of a changepoint exceeds a configurable threshold (default: 0.7), emit a `regime_shift_detected` event containing: stream name, timestamp, confidence, old regime summary statistics, new regime preliminary statistics, and recommended actions.

4. **Multi-stream correlation.** Support monitoring multiple streams simultaneously. When changepoints are detected in multiple correlated streams within a configurable time window (default: 60 seconds), emit a `correlated_regime_shift` event that links the individual detections, suggesting a common root cause.

5. **Policy recalibration hooks.** When a regime shift is detected, trigger configurable policy recalibration actions: adjust admission budgets, modify monitoring intervals, escalate alerting thresholds, or trigger diagnostic deep-scans (VOI-budgeted per bd-2nt).

6. **False positive control.** Implement a minimum run-length filter: changepoints are only signaled if the new regime persists for at least `min_run_length` observations (default: 10). This prevents spurious alerts from transient spikes.

7. **Regime history log.** Maintain a bounded history of detected regimes with their start/end timestamps, summary statistics, and confidence scores. This history is queryable for post-incident analysis.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. BOCPD algorithm implemented in `crates/franken-node/src/connector/bocpd.rs` with constant, geometric, and custom hazard functions.
2. All three observation models (Gaussian, Poisson, Categorical) implemented and tested.
3. Changepoint detection correctly identifies regime shifts in synthetic test streams with < 5% false positive rate and < 10% false negative rate on reference benchmarks.
4. Multi-stream correlation links concurrent changepoints within the configured window.
5. Policy recalibration hooks are invoked on regime shift detection.
6. Minimum run-length filter suppresses transient false positives.
7. Regime history log is queryable and bounded (configurable max entries, default: 1000).
8. Verification script `scripts/check_bocpd.py` with `--json` flag confirms all criteria.
9. Evidence artifacts written to `artifacts/section_10_11/bd-3u4/`.

## Key Dependencies

- bd-2nt (VOI-budgeted monitoring) — regime shifts can trigger diagnostic deep-scans.
- 10.13 telemetry namespace — regime shift events must conform to telemetry schema.
- 10.13 stable error namespace — detection errors use registered codes.
- Statistical libraries: `statrs` crate for probability distributions (or equivalent pure-Rust impl).

## Testing & Logging Requirements

- Unit tests covering each observation model's parameter update and predictive distribution.
- Integration tests with synthetic regime-shift streams (step change, gradual drift, periodic shift).
- Golden vector tests with known changepoint locations and expected detection times.
- Self-test mode that generates a synthetic stream with a known changepoint and confirms detection.
- Structured logging: `bocpd.observation`, `bocpd.changepoint_candidate`, `bocpd.regime_shift_detected`, `bocpd.correlated_shift`, `bocpd.false_positive_suppressed` events.

## Expected Artifacts

- `docs/specs/section_10_11/bd-3u4_contract.md` — specification document.
- `crates/franken-node/src/connector/bocpd.rs` — Rust implementation.
- `scripts/check_bocpd.py` — verification script.
- `tests/test_check_bocpd.py` — unit tests.
- `vectors/bocpd_regime_shifts.json` — golden test vectors.
- `artifacts/section_10_11/bd-3u4/verification_evidence.json` — evidence.
- `artifacts/section_10_11/bd-3u4/verification_summary.md` — summary.","acceptance_criteria":"AC for bd-3u4:
1. Implement a BOCPD (Bayesian Online Changepoint Detection) regime detector that processes streaming workload metrics and incident event streams to detect distributional shifts (regime changes) in real time.
2. The detector maintains a run-length distribution P(r_t | x_{1:t}) updated incrementally with each new observation; the underlying predictive model uses a conjugate-exponential hazard function with configurable prior parameters (hazard_lambda, prior_mean, prior_variance).
3. A regime change is signaled when the posterior probability of run-length r=0 (new regime) exceeds a configurable threshold (default: 0.5); the signal includes the estimated changepoint timestamp, confidence score, and pre/post regime summary statistics.
4. The detector supports multiple concurrent streams (e.g., request_rate, error_rate, latency_p99) with independent run-length distributions; a meta-detector can fuse per-stream signals into a joint regime-change verdict.
5. Detection latency is bounded: the detector must signal a regime change within K observations of the true changepoint (K configurable, default: 10) on synthetic step-function and ramp-function test signals.
6. Memory is bounded: the run-length distribution is truncated at a configurable max_run_length (default: 500) to prevent unbounded growth; truncation error is logged as BOCPD_TRUNCATION_WARNING when the tail probability exceeds 1e-4.
7. Unit tests verify: (a) step-function changepoint detected within K observations, (b) ramp-function changepoint detected within 2K observations, (c) stationary stream produces no false positives over 10,000 observations, (d) truncation at max_run_length does not cause missed detections on standard test signals, (e) multi-stream fusion correctly requires majority agreement.
8. Deterministic lab runtime (bd-2ko) scenario fixtures exercise: sudden load spike, gradual degradation, and oscillating workload patterns.
9. Structured log events: BOCPD_REGIME_CHANGE / BOCPD_OBSERVATION / BOCPD_TRUNCATION_WARNING with stream_id, run_length, posterior_probability, and trace correlation ID.","notes":"Plan-space QA addendum (2026-02-20):
1) Preserve full canonical-plan scope; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
2) Completion requires comprehensive verification coverage appropriate to scope: unit tests, integration tests, and E2E scripts/workflows with detailed structured logging (stable event/error codes + trace correlation IDs).
3) Completion requires reproducible evidence artifacts (machine-readable + human-readable) that allow independent replay and root-cause triage.
4) Any implementation PR for this bead must include explicit links to the above test/logging artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverMeadow","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:50.306945145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-21T01:31:05.016877390Z","closed_at":"2026-02-21T01:31:05.016842235Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-11","self-contained","test-obligations"]}
 {"id":"bd-3u6o","title":"[10.15] Enforce canonical virtual transport fault harness (from `10.14`) for distributed control protocols.","description":"## Why This Exists
Distributed control protocols (cross-node rollout coordination, remote fencing, epoch barrier participation, distributed saga steps) must be tested under realistic network fault conditions — packet drops, reordering, corruption, and partitions. Section 10.14 (bd-2qqu) built a canonical virtual transport fault harness that simulates these conditions deterministically by seed. This bead enforces that canonical harness on franken_node's distributed control protocols, making it a CI requirement that these protocols produce correct outcomes (or deterministic failures) under all seed-controlled fault scenarios. The product layer must not build its own fault injection — it must adopt the canonical harness.

## What This Must Do
1. Author `docs/testing/control_virtual_transport_faults.md` defining:
   - Which distributed control protocols are tested (remote fencing, cross-node rollout coordination, epoch barrier participation, distributed saga steps).
   - The fault classes injected: DROP (message lost), REORDER (messages arrive out of order), CORRUPT (message bytes altered), PARTITION (bidirectional communication failure).
   - The deterministic seed model: same seed -> same fault injection sequence -> same protocol behavior.
   - Expected protocol behaviors under each fault class (retry, abort, compensate, fail-closed).
2. Implement `tests/harness/control_virtual_transport_faults.rs` that:
   - Uses the canonical 10.14 virtual transport fault harness (bd-2qqu).
   - For each distributed protocol, runs it under each fault class with multiple seeds.
   - Asserts protocol correctness: either the protocol completes correctly despite the fault, or it fails deterministically with an appropriate error and compensation.
   - Captures protocol decisions and failures in a structured report.
3. Generate `artifacts/10.15/control_fault_harness_summary.json` with: per-protocol, per-fault-class, per-seed results (seed, fault_class, protocol_outcome, correctness_assertion, pass/fail).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Canonical harness scenarios are deterministic by seed, adopted by control-plane gates, and reproduce distributed protocol decisions and failures.
- The product layer uses the canonical 10.14 harness, not custom fault injection.
- Every distributed protocol is tested under all four fault classes (DROP, REORDER, CORRUPT, PARTITION).
- Same seed always produces the same protocol outcome.
- The fault harness summary is consumed by the section gate (bd-20eg).

## Testing & Logging Requirements
- **Unit tests**: Validate harness integration with a trivial request/response protocol under each fault class.
- **Integration tests**: Full fault injection on remote fencing protocol. Full injection on epoch barrier protocol.
- **Conformance tests**: Assert the harness covers all distributed protocols identified in the workflow inventory (bd-2177).
- **Adversarial tests**: Run a protocol with a seed that triggers maximum message reordering; assert correct compensations. Run with a partition that splits during an epoch barrier; assert deterministic abort.
- **Structured logs**: Event codes `VTF-001` (fault harness started for protocol), `VTF-002` (fault injected — drop/reorder/corrupt/partition), `VTF-003` (protocol completed correctly under fault), `VTF-004` (protocol failed correctly under fault — deterministic failure), `VTF-005` (protocol produced incorrect result under fault — gate failure). Include protocol_name, seed, fault_class, and trace correlation ID.

## Expected Artifacts
- `docs/testing/control_virtual_transport_faults.md`
- `tests/harness/control_virtual_transport_faults.rs`
- `artifacts/10.15/control_fault_harness_summary.json`
- `artifacts/section_10_15/bd-3u6o/verification_evidence.json`
- `artifacts/section_10_15/bd-3u6o/verification_summary.md`

## Dependencies
- **Upstream**: bd-2qqu (10.14 — canonical virtual transport fault harness)
- **Downstream**: bd-20eg (section gate)","acceptance_criteria":"- Canonical harness scenarios are deterministic by seed, adopted by control-plane gates, and reproduce distributed protocol decisions and failures.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:00.871954887Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T02:14:31.049763672Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T02:14:31.049732935Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-15","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3u6o","depends_on_id":"bd-2qqu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3u9es","title":"Fix Tokio bootstrap documentation drift after runtime shell removal","description":"Authoritative docs still say frankenengine-node uses a direct tokio dependency and tokio main, but crates/franken-node/Cargo.toml and crates/franken-node/src/main.rs no longer do.  {"id":"bd-3ua7","title":"[10.13] Implement sandbox profile system (`strict`, `strict_plus`, `moderate`, `permissive`) with policy compiler.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.13 — FCP Deep-Mined Expansion Execution Track (9I)

Why This Exists:
Deep connector/provider contract track: lifecycle FSMs, conformance harnesses, fencing, sandboxing, revocation freshness, and protocol hardening.

Task Objective:
Implement sandbox profile system (`strict`, `strict_plus`, `moderate`, `permissive`) with policy compiler.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Profile compiler emits enforceable low-level policy for each tier; profile downgrade attempts are blocked by policy; profile selection is auditable.

Expected Artifacts:
- `src/security/sandbox_policy_compiler.rs`, `docs/specs/sandbox_profiles.md`, `artifacts/10.13/sandbox_profile_compiler_output.json`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_13/bd-3ua7/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_13/bd-3ua7/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.13] Implement sandbox profile system (`strict`, `strict_plus`, `moderate`, `permissive`) with policy compiler.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.13] Implement sandbox profile system (`strict`, `strict_plus`, `moderate`, `permissive`) with policy compiler." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.13] Implement sandbox profile system (`strict`, `strict_plus`, `moderate`, `permissive`) with policy compiler." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.13] Implement sandbox profile system (`strict`, `strict_plus`, `moderate`, `permissive`) with policy compiler." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.13] Implement sandbox profile system (`strict`, `strict_plus`, `moderate`, `permissive`) with policy compiler." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:52.120604764Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T11:12:22.101895921Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T11:12:22.101868630Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-13","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ua7","depends_on_id":"bd-b44","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3uf8","title":"Stabilize frankenengine-node all-target remote check under rch timeout budget","description":"Proactive local quality triage in /data/projects/franken_node reproduced an rch timeout on the required all-target check for the local crate. Command: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_windywaterfall_bd_5lag cargo check -p frankenengine-node --all-targets. Result: remote exit 137 after about 301.9s on worker vmi1156319. The run reached the local target crate phase (Checking frankenengine-node) but emitted no code diagnostics before timeout. Narrow the failing surface (for example --lib vs tests/examples or targeted modules), identify the dominant cause, and land the first fix or gate-splitting improvement with rch-backed evidence.","notes":"Evidence so far: (1) rch exec cargo check -p frankenengine-node --all-targets timed out with exit 137 after about 301.9s after reaching Checking frankenengine-node. (2) rch exec cargo check -p frankenengine-node --lib reproduced the same exit 137 after about 301.9s, so the timeout is not limited to tests/examples fanout. (3) rch status recent builds show plain cargo check -p frankenengine-node can complete in about 600.6s on the fleet, which indicates the current 300s cargo-check budget is materially below observed compile time for this crate. (4) static compile-surface signals: crates/franken-node/src totals about 263668 LOC, main.rs is 4143 lines, and main.rs previously duplicated most of the library module tree exported by lib.rs. (5) previous main.rs dedupe changes have since been superseded by committed/shared worktree state. (6) CobaltGorge validated export-surface gating for migration/testing/extended-surface modules, but default --lib still exceeded budget even after those cuts. (7) WindyWaterfall tools/mod.rs shard: kept benchmark_suite, counterfactual_replay, evidence_replay_validator, and replay_bundle on the default surface; gated the remaining tools::* modules behind extended-surfaces after static consumer analysis showed no non-tools in-repo users outside main.rs. (8) fresh rch validation for the tools shard used env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch_windywaterfall_bd_3uf8_tools_v2 cargo check -p frankenengine-node --lib --message-format short and still exited 137 after about 303.1s with no new Rust diagnostics before timeout. (9) One intermediate rch run surfaced engine_dispatcher.rs _cleanup_guard missing, but the live shared worktree already carried a temp_dir-based edit there; treat that result as stale-hash noise unless it reproduces on a fresh run. (10) Current conclusion: multiple safe export-surface trims are structurally plausible and some are validated, but even combined they have not yet pulled the required default lib build under the effective remote timeout budget.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"WindyWaterfall","created_at":"2026-03-07T05:19:09.984774064Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-08T04:53:57.985132406Z","closed_at":"2026-03-08T04:53:57.985107319Z","close_reason":"Resolved on current shared worktree.
